response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine geospatial domain exposure (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G) and low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second, which maps loosely onto the pipeline and performance demands of this role. However, his modern C++ experience is a decade old and brief, he has no Rust, and his recent career (Google, Meta, RIDE) is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product and web work rather than routing, navigation, or map-data pipelines. He could plausibly grow into the role given his systems background, but he does not meet the core C++/navigation requirements today.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS experience: founded and built an open-source geo-data platform used in production, plus a FOSS4G talk
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching) in DLang, a systems language close to C++ in spirit
Broad, adaptable engineer with strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD) and comfort learning new stacks
concerns: Modern C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint in 2014-2015; no Rust, and recent work is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js
No experience with routing engines, navigation guidance, or map-tile/routing-data pipelines specifically
Career trajectory trends toward full-stack/web product work, raising questions about depth in OS-level and distributed-systems fundamentals the role demands
